name: wilson-resume
description: Persistence + re-arm companion for Claude Code's NATIVE `/goal` command (CC v2.1.139+). Native `/goal` sets a completion condition and keeps Claude working turn-to-turn until met — but the condition is session-scoped with NO disk persistence, so a hard interruption (usage limit / rate-limit / crash / a closed terminal never cleanly `--resume`d) loses it: the next session has no idea a goal was ever active. wilson-resume is the small cog that closes that gap. On every Stop it scans the transcript for the most recent `/goal` command (a user message `<command-name>/goal</command-name>` + `<command-args>CONDITION</command-args>`) and persists the condition to a per-project file at <DATA>/<sha1(project)>.json; a bare `/goal` (empty args = the native clear) drops it. On SessionStart (source startup or clear — `resume` already carries the native goal over, `compact` is same-session) it injects a short `## Goal — interrupted` block naming the condition and the exact `/goal <condition>` line to re-arm it. Inert unless a `/goal` was actually used. Does not re-implement `/goal` — only persists what `/goal` cannot. /wilson-resume status|show|clear|path. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_RESUME=1.
